Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting Plaintiff From: 1) Taking The Deposition Of Rcas Person Most Knowledgeable, And 2) Requiring Production Of Documents By Rcas Custodian Of Records As Designated In Plaintiffs Deposition Notice; Or Alternatively 3) Limiting Scope Of Deposition Notice
Off Calendar Sep-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Robert Setnik
Molly B. McKay, Esq., of GORDON & REES (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / opposing party RCA Corporation

RULING:
DEFENDANT RCA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER PROHIBITING PLAINTIFF FROM: 1) TAKING THE DEPOSITION OF RCA'S PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE, AND 2) REQUIRING PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS BY RCAS CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS AS DESIGNATED IN PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION NOTICE; OR ALTERNATIVELY 3) LIMITING SCOPE OF DEPOSITION NOTICE

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant RCA Corporation's motion for protective order prohibiting plaintiff from taking the deposition of its person most knowledgeable and requiring production of documents is denied and its alternate motion for protective order limiting the scope of the deposition is granted. The deposition is limited to the dates listed on Exhibit A of plaintiff's deposition notice and to televisions and radios. "Asbestos-containing products" is not a separate category. Defendant must produce a deponent and documents by October 7, 2011. If the deponent lacks knowledge as to some or all areas after making a reasonable effort to obtain such knowledge, the deponent can so state. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Defendant's position did not lack substantial justification. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
